era: 1990-present. Hard Rock. E=8. Capitol Hill, Seattle. Epic Records. 35+ years continuous. The grunge era's most commercially successful survivor.
associated_musicians: Andrew Wood (Mother Love Bone vocalist; died March 19 1990)
Chris Cornell (Temple of the Dog; Soundgarden)
Jack Irons (passed demo tape to Gossard; former RHCP)
key_albums: Ten (1991, Epic) -- 13 million US; Alive, Even Flow, Black, Jeremy
Vs. (1993, Epic) -- 950,000 debut week; Go, Rearviewmirror
Vitalogy (1994, Epic) -- vinyl first; Spin the Black Circle Grammy
No Code (1996) / Yield (1998) -- deliberate commercial retreat
Riot Act (2002) -- Bushleaguer, most political record
Dark Matter (2024, Andrew Watt) -- most recent statement

theory_nodes.concept: THE FINAL ASCENT OPENS. Pearl Jam at E=8 opens the engine's last ramp: 341 (E=8) → 342 (E=8) → 343 (E=9) → 344 (E=10). FIRST E=10 IN ENGINE HISTORY at degree 344. The ascent begins here.
theory_nodes.description: E=8 opens the final escalation to E=10.

theory_nodes.concept: Green River → Mother Love Bone → Pearl Jam genealogy: Gossard and Ament through two dissolved bands before Pearl Jam. Deep Six (1986) contains Green River. Andrew Wood's death ended Mother Love Bone before commercial moment. Temple of the Dog memorial introduced Vedder. Full genealogy documented.
theory_nodes.description: The most complete Seattle genealogy arc for any S36 entry.

theory_nodes.concept: Alive (Ten, 1991): Vedder's autobiographical account of discovering his biological father was not his stepfather, and that his biological father was dead before Vedder knew the truth. The song's chorus 'I'm still alive' is not triumph -- it is the shock of survival in the absence of the person who would have shared it.
theory_nodes.description: The autobiographical core of Pearl Jam's most important song.

theory_nodes.concept: Ticketmaster confrontation (1994): Pearl Jam filed a complaint with the US Department of Justice alleging Ticketmaster's monopoly practices harmed consumers and artists. DOJ investigated; no action taken. Pearl Jam attempted to tour without Ticketmaster venues; found insufficient accessible venues for their audience size. Failed commercially but documented as the most serious industry challenge to Ticketmaster from a major act in the 1990s.
theory_nodes.description: The fan-advocacy confrontation that defined Pearl Jam's extra-musical legacy.

theory_nodes.concept: Official bootleg series (2000-present): hundreds of official concert recordings released. Ten Club fan club (est. 1990). The most intentionally cultivated fan relationship of any grunge-era band.
theory_nodes.description: Pearl Jam's structural commitment to their audience as a distinguishing institutional practice.

cross_references.relationship: Direct predecessor. Stone Gossard and Jeff Ament in Green River before Pearl Jam. Green River is the genetic material; Pearl Jam is the commercial apex of the Gossard-Ament thread.

Immediate predecessor. Andrew Wood's death ended MLB before commercial moment. Temple of the Dog memorial project introduced Vedder to the musicians. Pearl Jam is what happened after MLB's dissolution.

Both at E=8. Melvins E=8 through sludge metal; Pearl Jam E=8 through hard rock. Melvins influenced the pre-grunge underground; Pearl Jam is the commercial expression of the grunge generation. Same energy, different trajectories.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. FINAL ASCENT OPENS. Pearl Jam's hard rock convergence (Gossard + Ament + McCready + Vedder + Cameron, five musicians at full commitment) paired with the river valley convergence (elk herd + fall storm + eagle on thermal). Multi-source convergence on both tracks at E=8.
